What challenges await the UK's incoming Prime Minister Andy Burnham?
items.description: The UK will be getting a new prime minister on July 20. And many challenges await Andy Burnham. He will be faced with a tepid economy, high government borrowing costs, and irregular migrants arriving in small boats that have fuelled support for Reform. In his address to the Labour party, he has vowed to boost the construction of public housing to try to resolve the homelessness crisis, and pump resources into social care. FRANCE 24's Rochelle Ferguson-Bouyahi looks at what awaits the incoming UK prime minister.

items.title: ‘Not a big deal’: New Venice mayor on US ambassador’s superyacht visit
items.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Venice's new mayor, Simone Venturini, just 38 years old and one month into office. He offers a unique governing philosophy rooted in balancing contradictions rather than embracing absolutes. His central argument is that Venice cannot be understood merely as an open-air museum threatened by mass tourism, nor simply as a global cultural icon. Instead, he portrays it as a living, breathing city that must simultaneously preserve its fragile heritage, remain open to international exchange, and reinvent its economic future.

items.description: Donald Trump has revived his long-running claims that the US election in 2020 was "fabricated". In a primetime address he accused China of what he called the "largest election data breach in history" and has released documents that he says reveal foreign interference. But intelligence officials say there is no evidence that foreign actors changed the outcome of the election.

items.title: Top U.S. prosecutor will not dispute DOJ decision to drop Gautam Adani's criminal case
items.description: U.S. District Judge Nicholas Garaufis ‌had asked U.S. Attorney Joseph Nocella Jr. to explain whether he agreed or disagreed with Mr. McCotter’s reasons for dropping the Gautam Adani case

items.title: The EU climate policy the world copied is under fire — what's at stake?
items.description: The EU Commission has proposed to weaken Europe's emissions trading system, a global blueprint for pricing carbon and cutting CO2. How effective is the scheme and where does it fall short?

items.title: Man charged over law firm fire and tobacco shooting in Melbourne's west
items.description: Police have made an arrest over an alleged arson in Spotswood and laid further charges over a shooting outside a tobacco store in Altona North.

items.title: ‘Our bodies contain poison’: Landmark trial in Argentina probes dangers of agrochemicals
items.description: Trial over agrochemical spraying in the city of Pergamino has set an unprecedented legal benchmark: for the first time, a federal court placed the entire agricultural production chain in the dock, from farmers and agronomists to public officials. Trial ends in acquittal for nearly all those excused, except two municipal employees. Leer más

items.title: China has stockpiled 'huge quantities' of frozen beef for this moment
items.description: As imports from Australia hit a wall because of large tariffs, China turns to its historically high stockpile of frozen beef.

items.title: Trump alleges China meddled in 2020 election and questions voting security ahead of midterms
items.description: China said Trump's claims were fabricated and US Democrats said he was paving the way to undermine November's elections.

items.title: "Shangwe, le bal": a dance project full of joy
items.description: On a sweltering afternoon in the French city of Lyon, around 60 dancers fully ignored the heat to immerse themselves in a festive performance brought to them by Kenyan-American choreographer and dancer Wanjiru Kamuyu.

items.title: SpaceX abruptly scrubs Starship test flight
items.description: The flight was the first mission planned by Elon Musk after SpaceX went public on Wall Street in June, with a record initial public offering of $135

items.title: Billionaire US envoy faces Venice protests amid yacht tour
items.description: Hundreds protested the arrival of billionaire and Trump-appointed US Ambassador Tilman Fertitta in Venice. The ambassador's stop — on his personal yacht — is part of a tour marking 250 years of American independence.

items.title: Ukraine lawmakers approve Koretskyi as new PM amid protests
items.description: It is President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's second Cabinet shakeup in a year. He faces criticism for dismissing reformist Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov.

items.title: How the new U.S. visa rules affect your online privacy | Explained
items.description: Privacy experts have slammed the U.S. government’s new social media vetting requirements, likening the country’s approach to that of an authoritarian regime. However, they note it is difficult to claim that privacy laws are being broken as visa applicants can choose to ignore the request, though they put their visa application at risk by doing so

items.title: What this desert city can teach the world about tackling heat deaths
items.description: Maricopa County in Arizona has some of the hottest temperatures, but it has reduced the number of heat-related deaths.

items.title: From colonial symbol to sanctuary: Paris's Grand Mosque turns 100
items.description: The Grand Mosque of Paris, built in 1926 in the Latin Quarter as a tribute to Muslim soldiers who died for France during World War I, marks its 100th anniversary this year. French historian Benjamin Stora traces its little-known role as a geopolitical bridge – not only between France and Algeria, but also between Muslims and Jews.

items.title: Rubio says United States wants to globalise fight against far-left 'terrorism'
items.description: Argentina among nations represented at meeting on "The Resurgence of Political Terrorism" hosted by US Secretary of State Marco Rubio. Leer más

items.title: Running dry: How to store more groundwater for dry seasons
items.description: More frequent weather extremes are leading to water shortages during droughts and flooding during heavy rain. But groundwater storage can help secure water for dry seasons while mitigating extreme rain.

items.title: Police set to brief Donald locals about alleged child murder
items.description: Victoria Police will hold a public meeting in Donald on Monday to update locals on their investigation into the alleged murder of 13-year-old Layla Jeffery.

items.title: Europe's early heat wave led to rise in deaths, may have killed over 10,000
items.description: Several heat waves have killed thousands of people in the past few years in Europe

items.title: Could Russia be about to lose its grip on chess leadership?
items.description: Russians have long pulled the strings when it comes to the governing body of international chess. But now two Germans are vying for FIDE's top job.

items.title: Mandela Day: What his legacy means in today’s South Africa
items.description: As South Africa grapples with inequality and division, Mandela Day has taken on new political significance.

items.title: British Embassy playfully defuses Argentina-England match tensions
items.description: Embassy's Instagram account shares a spoof "internal memo" instructing its community manager on how to behave – win or lose – in the wake of Wednesday’s World Cup semi-final. Leer más

items.title: How new PM Andy Burnham will change UK's international role
items.description: From northwest England to the global stage: Andy Burnham becomes Britain's new prime minister on Monday. How will the former mayor of Manchester tackle international affairs?

items.title: Germany massively expands federal police powers
items.description: The German government has increased the powers of its federal police, especially in the use of AI, surveillance and drone defenses. But experts warn of constitutional challenges.

items.title: Kolkata sings for Messi as World Cup fever takes hold
items.description: South Asia’s greatest football city has united behind its footballing saint.

items.title: 'Works every time': Superstition ramps up ahead in Argentina of World Cup final
items.description: Football fans are putting their faith in good luck rituals ahead of Sunday's World Cup final. But these wacky habits are no joke to those who practice them. Leer más

items.title: Amid elections, Sao Tome and Principe balances global powers
items.description: As voters head to the polls, Sao Tome and Principe is attracting international scrutiny. A military agreement with Russia has heightened interest in the strategically located archipelago.

items.title: Argentina v England: Much more than just a game
items.description: From the South Atlantic war to Diego Maradona's famous goals, the semi-final against England has ramifications far beyond the pitch. Leer más

items.title: UN launches new refugee hub in Ethiopia offering legal aid and protection
items.description: In tonight's edition, Algeria mourns the 11 people killed in a deadly orphanage fire as investigators search for the cause. Ethiopia and the UN refugee agency launch a new support centre offering refugees free legal advice. Meanwhile, Drill music is booming in Ethiopia, winning over young fans while fuelling debate over its place in a conservative society.

items.title: West Indies cricket legend Sir Garfield Sobers dies
items.description: Sir Garfield Sobers, considered one of the game's greatest all-rounders, has died aged 89. The International Cricket Council said he was one of the "greatest cricketers to have ever graced the game."

items.title: Lawyers clash in Maradona death trial, forcing hearing to halt
items.description: Judges suspend proceedings and warn they could remove counsel from the case after a heated confrontation between rival lawyers nearly turns physical. Leer más

items.title: Zoox recalls self-driving cars because they may not detect smoke
Magnitude 5.0 earthquake hits southeastern Turkiye: disaster agency
items.description: Turkiye is crisscrossed by several geological fault lines that have previously caused catastrophes in the country.

items.title: Lasaco Assurance turns tide on half-year loss, helped by cost efficiency
items.description: Its return to profitability owed not so much to revenue growth as to cost cutting, as insurance revenue, its core income source, retreated by 3.2 per cent from the half-year 2025 level to N16.3 billion. The post Lasaco Assurance turns tide on half-year loss, helped by cost efficiency appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Milei almost halved education and science spending, reports finds
items.description: University of Buenos Aires researchers say education investment has almost halved in two years since President Javier Milei took office, while second Grupo EPC analysis says funding for science has fallen by more than 46%. Leer más

items.title: Paris region's record June heatwave more than doubled normal mortality rate to reach 3,000
items.description: Authorities in the Paris region recorded "1,565 more deaths than expected" over a one-week period in June as the country grappled with a protracted heatwave that broke all-time temperature records, France's public health agency said in a report released on Friday, adding that people over 65 accounted for more than 80 percent of fatalities.

items.title: Space start-up launches India’s first commercial rocket designed for orbit
items.description: India's first space-tech unicorn Skyroot Aerospace successfully launched Vikram-1 on Saturday.

items.title: Thousands protest in Kyiv for second day over ouster of popular defence minister Fedorov
items.description: Protesters rallied for a second day in the Ukrainian capital on Friday, angered by President Volodymyr Zelensky's decision to fire youthful defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov, seen as an innovator who pioneered the country’s successful drone warfare but clashed with the traditional military establishment.

items.title: Court dismisses suit seeking INEC’s recognition for Wabara, Turaki as PDP leaders
items.description: The judge, who affirmed the leadership of Mohammed Abdulrahman-led PDP - the faction loyal to the FCT Minister, Nyesom Wike - said “the preliminary objections filed by all the defendants succeed.” The post Court dismisses suit seeking INEC’s recognition for Wabara, Turaki as PDP leaders appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Tensions as Guinea-Bissau freezes ties with Cape Verde over claims of interference
items.description: Praia earlier this week requested that Bissau swiftly release the country’s main opposition leader Domingos Simões Pereira.

items.title: Meta, Anthropic in talks for potential $10 billion compute lease deal, source says
Women sand miners toil stripped Cape Verde beach
items.description: On a rocky Cape Verde beach, Maria and Vania fill their buckets with black sand the moment that waves recede, transporting it on their heads to a stockpile where it will be sold illegally.

items.title: Boris Nadezhdin is a rare Russian politician speaking out against the Ukraine war
items.description: Boris Nadezhdin wants to run for Russia's parliament on a platform that gives voice to the Russians against the war in Ukraine. That's why, he says, the Kremlin is doing everything to stop him.

items.title: Philippines condemns monkey video on Chinese media as racist
items.description: The AI video on China Daily showed a monkey in Philippine garments singing its claims to the South China Sea.

items.description: Argentina captain added another chapter to his astonishing career on Wednesday, inspiring his team to victory in their semi-final against England. Leer más

items.description: U.S. Vice ‌President J.D. Vance says some members of the Israeli government tried to influence U.S. public opinion to oppose a deal by the U.S. to end the ⁠war with Iran

items.title: Orange haze from Canada wildfires seen in Ontario and northern US
items.description: There are currently 838 wildfires actively burning across the country, according to the Canadian Interagency Forest Fire Centre.

items.title: Iran threatens 'full-scale' offensive if US keeps bombing
items.description: An Iranian general warned no border will be safe if the US strikes continue past the next few days. Meanwhile, the US rejected Tehran's claims that two oil tankers exploded near the Strait of Hormuz. DW has the latest.

items.title: Israeli army destroys three schools in southern Lebanon, minister says
items.description: At least 20 schools have now been completely destroyed in southern Lebanon, with 100 more damaged.

items.title: World Cup 2026: Do supporters know the World Cup records?
items.description: Which is the only team to have competed in every World Cup? And which has won the most? Throughout the tournament, we asked fans about World Cup records.
